articles.title: Exosomes in cancer drug resistance: dual roles in therapy failure and emerging precision therapeutics
articles.summary: This review examines the dual roles of exosomes in cancer drug resistance, exploring how these extracellular vesicles contribute to therapy failure while simultaneously presenting opportunities for precision therapeutic development. The article discusses mechanistic pathways through which exosomes mediate treatment resistance and highlights emerging strategies to leverage exosome biology for improved cancer therapeutic outcomes.

articles.title: Risk of fracture with gabapentinoid treatment in older adults: a multinational population-based study
articles.summary: This multinational population-based study examines the association between gabapentinoid treatment and fracture risk in older adults. The research represents a collaborative effort across multiple countries and institutions to evaluate the safety profile of gabapentinoids, commonly prescribed medications in this population. The findings provide evidence relevant to assessing the benefit-risk profile of gabapentinoid use in older patients.

articles.summary: This study examines the structural characteristics of swine leukocyte antigen (SLA) class I molecules, which are important for understanding immune recognition in swine. articles.summary: Despite advances in protein structure prediction, how proteins fold remains unclear. Understanding protein (mis)folding will require single-molecule techniques and advanced computation tools.

articles.title: Targeted enzyme discovery using metal-coordination mining
articles.summary: A methodology for mining protein structure databases on the basis of the distinct intrinsic structures of metal-binding active sites in enzymes enables the discovery of new families of radical halogenases.

articles.title: EGFR-dependent delivery of phosphorylated IGFBP3 by cancer stem cells drives chemotherapy resistance in colorectal cancer
articles.summary: Cancer stem cells in colorectal cancer secrete phosphorylated IGFBP3 protein through an EGFR-dependent mechanism, which promotes chemotherapy resistance in tumor cells. This finding identifies a novel pathway by which cancer stem cells communicate with bulk tumor cells to reduce drug sensitivity. The study suggests that targeting this EGFR-IGFBP3 signaling axis may enhance chemotherapy efficacy in colorectal cancer.

articles.title: Defining safety parameters for first-trimester malaria treatment trials: stakeholder views from Real-Time Delphi survey
articles.summary: This study used a Real-Time Delphi survey to gather stakeholder perspectives on defining appropriate safety parameters for clinical trials evaluating malaria treatments during the first trimester of pregnancy. The research synthesizes expert consensus across multiple disciplines to establish evidence-based safety standards for this vulnerable patient population in treatment development.

articles.title: Beyond one-size-fits-all: Latent patient classes at methadone treatment entry and their clinical implications - Evidence from a large real-world cohort
articles.summary: This real-world cohort study identifies distinct patient subgroups at the point of methadone treatment initiation using latent class analysis, revealing heterogeneous clinical profiles that have implications for personalized treatment approaches. The findings challenge standardized one-size-fits-all treatment protocols and suggest that patient stratification at treatment entry may better inform clinical management strategies. The research provides evidence from a large cohort to support tailored interventions based on baseline patient characteristics in opioid use disorder treatment.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence and Protein Design: A retrospective study on 20-year emerging trends and core research areas from bibliometric perspectives
articles.summary: This bibliometric analysis examines 20 years of research trends at the intersection of artificial intelligence and protein design, identifying core research areas and emerging developments in the field. The study systematically maps the evolution of scientific publications and research focus areas to provide a retrospective overview of how AI applications have shaped protein design research. This comprehensive analysis offers insights into the trajectory and current landscape of AI-driven protein design innovation.

articles.title: Combination cardiometabolic therapy in type 2 diabetes: optimizing SGLT2 inhibitor and GLP‑1 receptor agonist use
articles.summary: This review examines the combined use of SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1 receptor agonists in managing type 2 diabetes, focusing on optimizing cardiometabolic outcomes through dual-agent therapy strategies.

articles.title: Non-Pharmacological Interventions for Postoperative Pain in Breast Cancer Patients: A Network Meta-analysis
articles.summary: This network meta-analysis evaluates the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for managing postoperative pain in breast cancer patients. The study synthesizes evidence from multiple treatment approaches to identify which interventions provide the most effective pain relief in this patient population. Results aim to inform clinical practice guidelines for postoperative pain management without relying solely on pharmaceutical approaches.

articles.title: Safety and efficacy of metformin for the treatment of pain: protocol for a systematic review
articles.summary: This protocol describes a planned systematic review to evaluate the safety and efficacy of metformin in treating pain. The review will synthesize evidence on metformin's use as a pain management intervention, with assessment of both therapeutic benefits and safety outcomes. This work addresses an emerging area in pain management research by examining the potential role of a widely-used diabetes medication in pain treatment.

articles.title: A multicentre randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial of benznidazole versus nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, alongside a prospective cohort: the BENBRASIL trial protocol
articles.summary: This protocol describes a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial comparing benznidazole and nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, integrated with a prospective cohort study. The BENBRASIL trial aims to evaluate the relative efficacy and safety of these two antiparasitic treatments in this patient population.

articles.title: Precision therapeutics for wound healing: Molecular targets, signalling networks, and advanced drug design strategies
articles.summary: This review examines precision therapeutic approaches for wound healing, focusing on molecular targets and signalling networks that drive the healing process. The article outlines advanced drug design strategies that leverage these mechanistic insights to develop targeted interventions for wound repair. The work synthesizes current understanding of protein-level mechanisms relevant to therapeutic development in this field.

articles.title: Augmenting Medical Judgment in Signal Confirmation: Design Considerations for an AI-Enabled Causality Assessment Framework
articles.summary: This paper presents design considerations for an artificial intelligence-enabled framework intended to support medical judgment in causality assessment during drug safety signal confirmation. The framework is developed to augment—rather than replace—human expertise in evaluating potential adverse drug reactions. The work addresses practical implementation aspects for integrating AI tools into pharmacovigilance processes.

articles.title: Evolution of KRAS molecular glues: from allele-selective to Pan-RAS and protein-protein interaction modulators
articles.summary: This review examines the development of KRAS-targeted molecular glues, tracing their evolution from allele-selective inhibitors to pan-RAS inhibitors and protein-protein interaction modulators. The article documents advances in targeting strategies for KRAS mutations, a significant focus in oncology drug development. The work synthesizes current approaches to modulating KRAS function through distinct molecular mechanisms.

articles.title: Primary tumor resection prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival in metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors: a systematic review and meta-analysis
articles.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrates that primary tumor resection performed prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival outcomes in patients with metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. This finding suggests a potential treatment sequencing benefit for this patient population. The analysis supports consideration of surgical resection as part of a multimodal treatment strategy before initiating peptide receptor radionuclide therapy.

articles.title: Pharmaceutical Engineering of Lipid-Based Nanocarriers for Cancer Nanomedicines: From Doxorubicin to mRNA Platforms
articles.summary: This review examines the pharmaceutical engineering of lipid-based nanocarriers for cancer treatment, covering applications ranging from conventional chemotherapy delivery (doxorubicin) to emerging mRNA-based therapeutic platforms. Lipid nanocarriers represent a critical drug delivery technology that enables targeted cancer nanomedicine through optimized formulation design. The article synthesizes current approaches to nanocarrier development and their clinical relevance across multiple cancer therapeutic modalities.

articles.title: Cardiovascular outcomes of empagliflozin-GLP-1RA combination therapy in type 2 diabetes: EMPRISE study
articles.summary: The EMPRISE study evaluated cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes patients receiving combination therapy with empagliflozin (an SGLT2 inhibitor) and GLP-1 receptor agonists. This pharmacoepidemiological research from Brigham and Women's Hospital examined the safety and efficacy profile of this drug combination in a real-world patient population. The study contributes evidence on whether dual therapy with these two drug classes provides cardiovascular benefits in diabetic patients.

articles.title: Formulation development and optimization of sparfloxacin-loaded solid lipid nanoparticles integrated with microneedles for the treatment of biofilm-infected wounds
articles.summary: Researchers developed and optimized solid lipid nanoparticles containing sparfloxacin, an antibiotic, integrated with microneedles as a delivery system for treating biofilm-infected wounds. This formulation approach combines nanoparticle technology with microneedle delivery to enhance therapeutic efficacy against biofilm infections.

articles.title: Machine Learning-Based PAT Tool for Real-Time Residual Moisture Monitoring in Lyophilized Biopharmaceuticals Using NIR Spectroscopy
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a machine learning-based process analytical technology (PAT) tool that uses near-infrared (NIR) spectroscopy to monitor residual moisture in real-time during lyophilization of biopharmaceutical products. The tool integrates machine learning algorithms with NIR spectroscopic data to enable non-destructive, continuous assessment of moisture content in freeze-dried biopharmaceuticals. This approach supports real-time process control and quality assurance during lyophilization manufacturing.

articles.summary: A study identifies calcium signaling pathway involvement in a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases, suggesting common molecular mechanisms underlying both disease categories.
